original: Battle of Nechtansmere/Dun Nechtain: Picts led by Brude MacBeli beat Northumbrians in Scotland, led by their king Ecgfrith who is killed
Second Battle of Lincoln fought near Lincoln, England, resulting in the defeat of Prince Louis of France by William Marshal, 2nd Earl of Pembroke
Earthquake strikes Kamakura, Japan, 30,000 killed
King Sancho IV of Castile creates the Study of General Schools of Alcalá
Treaty of Paris restores Gascony to the English and arranges marriage of English Prince Edward to French Princess Isabella
Rienzo calls Rome for people's tribunal
Portuguese explorer João da Nova Castell discovers the uninhabited Ascension Island in the South Atlantic Ocean
Ignatius Loyola seriously wounded by a cannonball
Duke of Albany leaves Scotland
Magdeburg in Germany seized by forces of the Holy Roman Empire under earl Johann Tilly, most inhabitants massacred, one of the bloodiest incidents of the Thirty Years' War
Dorchester Massachusetts forms 1st school funded by local taxes
Elias Neau forms school for slaves in NY
1st Jockey Club forms in South Carolina
The British pass the second of the Intolerable Acts: the Massachusetts Government Act, giving British-appointed governor wide-ranging powers
Citizens of Mecklenburg County, North Carolina declare independence from Great Britain
Britain and the Netherlands sign the Peace of Paris
Douglass Hyde receives the first US patent for a fountain pen
First railroad timetable is published in a newspaper (Baltimore American)
Otto is named the first modern king of Greece
York Minster badly damaged by fire
First legislative assembly convenes in Hawaii
HMS Erebus and HMS Terror with 134 men under John Franklin sail from the River Thames in England, beginning a disastrous expedition to find the Northwest Passage. All hands are lost.
Cornerstone of University of Washington laid in Seattle
Kentucky proclaims its neutrality in Civil War
North Carolina becomes 11th and last state to secede from the Union
US Marshals, in search of secessionist evidence, seize the previous year's telegraph dispatches
Battle at Ware Bottom Church, Virginia, 1,400 killed or injured
Republican National Convention, meets in Chicago, nominates Grant
Second Chamber of the Dutch Parliament abolishes capital punishment in The Netherlands
International Bureau of Weights & Measures formed by the signing of the Metre Convention treaty by 17 states and prototypes of the meter and the kilogram selected
Gotthard rail tunnel between Switzerland & Italy opens
George Sampson patents clothes dryer
First commercial movie performance is held at 153 Broadway, NYC
The six-ton chandelier of the Palais Garnier opera house in Paris falls on the crowd, resulting in the death of one person and injuries to many others
First speeding infraction by a New York cabbie driving an electric car - 12mph down Lexington Street
Second modern Summer Olympics (Games of the II Olympiad) opens in Paris and continues for five months
United States withdraws its troops from Cuba as the first Cuban president, Tomas Estrada Palma takes power
Britain's House of Commons begins a debate on the charges of poor administration and ill treatment of natives in Belgium's colony in the Congo Free State
Edwin Boaler Alletson hits 189 in 90 mins Notts v Sussex
Bataafsche Petroleum Me begins oil extraction of Maracaibo
Codell, Kansas hit by tornado (also on same date in 1917 & 1918)
Saturday Evening Post cover features Norman Rockwell painting
Turkish government authorizes Jews to return to Tel Aviv & Jaffa
US Navy launches USS New Mexico, its first battleship with an electric-turbine propulsion system
Policemen raid the Cubs' bleachers & arrest 24 fans for gambling
"Egypt" sinks off Ushant after colliding with "Seine" killing 90
Belgian government of Jaspar takes power
Congress passes Air Commerce Act, licensing of pilots & planes
Railway Labor Act became law in USA
Thomas Edisonsays Americans prefer silent movies over talkies
First airplane catapulted from a dirigible, piloted by Charles Nicholson
University of California dedicates $1,500 to research on prevention and cure of athlete's foot
Engelbert Dollfuss becomes chancellor of Austria
"3 Little Fishies" by Kay Kyser swam to #1
First regular transatlantic airmail is delivered by Pan Am from New York to Marseille, France
German General Guderian's tanks reach the English Channel (British expeditionary army)
Soccer team HZVV forms in Hoogeveen
Trailing 7-1 in 9th to Pitts, Phils win 8-7
Archer's "Christian Calendar & Gregorian Reform" published
Former Dutch PM Hendrikus Colijn says Dutch Indies not ready for independence
Germany begins airborne invasion of Crete
White Sox Taft Wright sets AL record of RBIs in 13 consecutive games
US Navy 1st permitted black recruits to serve
French, British and US victory parade in Tunis, Tunisia
US Communist Party dissolves
Cubs Claude Passeau makes his 1st error since September 21, 1941, ending pitcher's fielding record of 273 consecutive errorless chances
Cleveland Indians tie AL record of 18 walks (beat Red Sox 13-4)
Argentine parliament accepts separation of church and state
Atomic fusion (thermonuclear) bomb dropped from plane at Bikini Atoll
Jordan government of Samir resigns
US performs nuclear test at Enewetak Atoll (atmospheric tests)
Ford wins battle with Chrysler to call its new car "Falcon"
Japanese-Americans regain their citizenship
Yanks sink to last place, 1st time since May 25, 1940
Baseball game in Milwaukee postponed due to dense fog
Henzes opera "Elegy for Young Lovers," premieres in Schwetzingen
Mauritania adopts constitution
White mob attacks "Freedom Riders" in Montgomery, Alabama

Buster Mathis beats future world heavyweight champion Joe Frazer on points at trials in Flushing, NY to qualify for US Olympic boxing team; Mathis injures thumb, replaced by Frazier who wins gold medal
Pakistani Boeing 720-B crashes at Cairo Egypt, killing 121
Yorkshire all out for 23 v Hampshire at Middlesbrough

19th Cannes Film Festival: "A Man and a Woman" directed by Claude Lelouch and "The Birds, the Bees and the Italians" directed by Pietro Germi jointly awarded the Grand Prix du Festival International du Film
BBC bans the Beatles' song "A Day in the Life" due to the line "I'd love to turn you on" being construed as a drug reference
US troop capture Hill 937/Hamburger Hill in Vietnam
100,000 march in NY supporting US policies in Vietnam
2 die and 70 injured when 2 NYC subway trains crash in Queens
The Beatles' "Let it Be" documentary film directed by Michael Lindsay-Hogg, premieres at the Liverpool Gaumont Cinema and the London Pavilion; none of the group attend
Pentagon reports blacks constitute 11% of US soldiers in SE Asia
Republic of Cameroon declared as its constitution is ratified
2 motorcycle racers killed and a dozen injured in a crash during the 1973 Nations Grand Prix in Monza,Italy
USSR performs nuclear test at Sary Shagan USSR
Long-distance train service the Orient Express departs Paris on its last trip to Istanbul (began 1883)
Three PFLP members kill a police officer near El Al Airlines at Orly Airport in Paris
US launches Pioneer Venus 1; produces 1st global radar map of Venus
710 families in Love Canal area of Niagara Falls, New York are evacuated due to linger effects of prior use as chemical waste disposal site
Drummer Peter Criss quits rock band Kiss
Fire in nursing home in Kingston Jamaica, kills 157
In a referendum, 59.5% of Quebec voters reject separatism
William Finn's musical "March of the Falsettos" opens Off-Broadway in NYC
"Every Breath You Take" single released by The Police (Billboard Song of the Year, 1983)
38th Cannes Film Festival: "When Father Was Away on Business" directed by Emir Kusturica wins the Palme d'Or
Dow Jones Industrial Average closes above 1,300 for 1st time
FBI arrests John A Walker Jr, convicted of spying for USSR
Indians-Brewers game at Cleveland Stadium is 1st rain-out of 1985, ends record string of 458 ML games since Opening Day without a rain-out
Larry Holmesbeats Carl Williams in 15 for heavyweight boxing title
US began broadcasts to Cuba on Radio Marti
Flintstones 25th Anniversary Celebration airs on CBS-tv
Howard Stern fans disrupt rival radio station WMMR's morning DJ John DeBella's "Louie Louie" parade in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
China declares martial law in Beijing
Walter McConnel, 57, is oldest to reach 27,000' Mt Everest top
Hubble Space Telescope sends its first photographs from space
44th Cannes Film Festival: "Barton Fink" directed by Ethan and Joel Coen wins the Palme d'Or
Jeff Reardon gains his 300th career save
Soviet parliament approves law allowing citizens to travel abroad
American rapper Tung Twista raps 597 syllables in under 60 seconds
India launches its 1st satellite independently
10m meteor comes within 150,000 km of Earth (1993KA)
274th & final "Cheers" episode airs on NBC
Bobcat Goldthwait charged with misdemeanors for fire on The Tonight Show
Neak Sre (The Rice People), directed by Rithy Panh and starring Phan Peng, premieres at the Cannes Film Festival

CBS News fires co-anchor Connie Chung
49th Cannes Film Festival: "Secrets & Lies" directed by Mike Leigh wins the Palme d'Or

UN and Iraq agree to Resolution 986, which provides Iraq with the opportunity to sell $1 billion of oil for 90 days for a 180-day trial period; proceeds from the sale would be used for humanitarian purposes
Cosmos Zenit-2 Launch (Russia), Failed
Thor-2A Delta 2 Launch (Norway/USA), Successful
White Sox Frank Thomas reaches base safely for 15th straight time

The independence of East Timor is recognized by Portugal, formally ending 23 years of Indonesian rule and 3 years of provisional UN administration (Portugal itself was the former colonizer of East Timor until 1976)
Mexico is the first Latin American country to officially enter recession
6.0 magnitude earthquake kills 6 and injures dozens in northern Italy
Tomislav Nikolić elected president of Serbia
133 people are killed and 283 are injured in a continued wave of insurgency in Iraq
25 people are killed and over 200 injured by a EF-5 tornado in Moore, Oklahoma
Church of Scotland votes to allow openly gay men and women to be ministers
Two people are killed and 20 are injured after a hot air balloon crashes in Cappadocia, Turkey
Yahoo purchases Tumbler for $1.1 Billion
Five major world banks (JPMorgan, Barclays, Citigroup, RBS, and UBS) are fined US$5.7 billion for manipulating currency markets, one of the largest fines ever
Journal Nature publishes findings of the oldest human-made tools from Kenya’s Turkana Basin, dating back 3.3 million years, the first to predate the Homo genus
NASCAR 2016 Hall of Fame inductees: Bobby Isaac, Terry Labonte and Jerry Cook
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, Greystone G &CC: Spaniard Miguel Ángel Jiménez wins by 3 from American trio Joe Durant, Steve Stricker & Gene Sauers
Two cyclists attacked by a cougar, 1 dies, in North Bend, Washington State
Bangladesh imposes a 65-day ban on coastal fishing to conserve fish stocks
Google stops support for Huawei's Android system in an escalation of the tech war between US and China
Cyclone Amphan comes ashore in West Bengal, East India and Bangladesh, with winds of 185 km per hour (115 mph) killing at least 84 people
WHO reports most new cases in a single day - 106,000 worldwide of COVID-19
Israel and Hamas agree to a bilateral ceasefire in Gaza after nearly two weeks of fighting, amid international diplomatic efforts
One of Sri Lanka's worst ecological disasters as cargo ship MS X-Press Pearl, carrying toxic chemicals, catches fire off the country's coast and begins spilling debris
English ITV This Morning TV presenter Phillip Schofield resigns after 20 years in the role. It later emerges he was having an affair with a younger colleague and lied about it.
Common cockroach originated in South Asia, according to new study, then domesticated in Germany before spreading around the world
Record set for world's most expensive feather - a huia feather from an extinct New Zealand bird sells for $46,521 NZD (about $28,400 USD) at auction in Auckland
UK Public Enquiry report into the infected blood scandal  that killed around 3,000 people between 1970 and 1991, published blaming the government and the health service
"Heart Lamp" by Manu Mushtaq and translator Deepa Bhasthi wins the 2025 International Booker Prize award

Alpine village of Blatten, Switzerland evacutes nearly 300 people, 90 sheep, 26 cows (including an injured cow that needed to be ferried out by helicopter), and 20 domestic rabbits, due to concerns of a glacier moving at an alarming pace
First Council of Nicaea - 1st ecumenical council of Christian bishops at Nicaea, Asia Minor
French KingCharles VIIIleaves Naples
Portuguese explorerVasco da Gamaarrives in Calicut, India, becoming the first European to reach India by sea
English KingHenry VIIIgrants a charter to the Corporation of Trinity House to regulate pilotage on the river Thames in London (still in existence)
William Shakespeare's Sonnets are first published in London, perhaps illicitly, by publisher Thomas Thorpe
England passes Act of Grace, forgiving followers ofJames II
Charles Xbecomes King of France

Henrik Ibsen's play "Ghosts" (Gengangere) premieres in Chicago

Mexican PresidentVenustiano Carranza, under attack by American petroleum companies, faces an armed rebellion by right-wing Sonoro triumvirate after nationalizing subsoil rights

At 7:40 AM,Charles Lindberghtakes off from New York to cross the Atlantic for Paris aboard the Spirit of St. Louis in the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight
Treaty of Jeddah is signed between the United Kingdom andIbn Saud, recognizing the independence of the Kingdom of Hejaz and Nejd, the forerunner of Saudi Arabia

Sukarnoappointed president of Indonesia

Elton Johnis the 1st western pop star to tour USSR
American heavyweight boxerLarry Holmesbeats countryman Tim Witherspoon by split decision to retain his WBC title at the Dunes Hotel & Casino, Las Vegas
Michael Dokes &Mike Weaverfight to a draw in 15 for heavyweight boxing title

ActressScarlett Johanssonsays OpenAI tried to hire her voice and when she refused created a synthetic voice, known as Sky, for an update to ChatGPT very similar to hers
Lai Ching-te is sworn in as the 8th President of Taiwan, after his predecessorTsai Ing-wenstepped down after eight years
Chess grandmasterMagnus Carlsenplays to a draw against an online team of 143,000 players from around the world
